description: An examination of the basic principles of fisheries ecology and population dynamics and how these are used as the basis for stock assessment and fisheries management. Topics include: current issues in BC, Canadian and global fisheries; growth, mortality, reproduction and recruitment; stock-recruitment and age-structured fisheries models; fisheries management tactics and strategies.
preOrCorequisites:
• Complete 1 of the following
    • Completed or concurrently enrolled in 1 of:
      • BIOL330 - Study Design and Data Analysis (1.5)
      • BIOL370 - Conservation Biology (1.5)
      • ES320 - Conservation Biology (1.5)
      • ES344 - Design and Analysis for Environmental Science (1.5)
    • or permission of the department.
title: Fisheries Ecology and Management
